mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2005 retail sales at 526540M suggests sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to precede positive SPX returns in > 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This elevated rate environment compresses equity valuations and increases discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.83% as of April 2005, approaching tightening threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 3.7% signals tight labor markets and wage pressure, which increases corporate cost structures and reduces profit margins. This cost inflation drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% in April 2005, already below threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of instances when UNRATE < 3.7%, or Rank IC between UNRATE and 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) and employment growth (PAYEMS) jointly signal robust supply-side capacity utilization. When both series show month-over-month acceleration, the economy is operating near potential output, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 108.67 and PAYEMS at 151945K in April 2005 suggest strong production and employment
failure_condition: Joint acceleration in INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to precede positive 20-day SPX returns in > 45% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling a sharp contraction in residential investment demand. This demand shock propagates through construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1256K in March 2005, baseline for detecting sharp contractions
regime_hint: demand shock or housing cycle downturn
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances following > 10% HOUST declines, or correlation between HOUST shocks and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below inflation, creating negative real rates. This regime signals monetary accommodation that is losing credibility, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as inflation expectations rise and bond yields spike.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY growth
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 257.27 and FEDFUNDS at 1.83% in April 2005
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 50% of instances when trigger is active, or interaction coefficient between inflation and real rate gap is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month, signaling labor market weakness and reduced consumer income growth. This deceleration precedes negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151945K in April 2005, baseline for detecting deceleration
regime_hint: labor market slowdown or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when PAYEMS growth < 100K, or Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate quarter-over-quarter, signaling broad-based demand expansion. This joint acceleration in aggregate and consumer demand drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved earnings visibility and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19112.54B and RSAFS at 526540M in Q1 2005 suggest sustained expansion
failure_condition: Joint acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to precede positive 60-day SPX returns in > 45% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).
